title: Delhi horror: Woman allegedly gangraped in private bus, 2 arrested
content: Delhi Police have registered a case and arrested two individuals after a woman alleged gangrape in a private bus. The bus used in the incident has been seized, and authorities are conducting a thorough investigation into the matter from all possible angles.

title: 'It’s going to finish one day': Virat Kohli makes honest reflection
content: Virat Kohli delivered a masterful performance, smashing his ninth IPL century to lead Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a successful chase of 193 against Kolkata Knight Riders. This historic innings saw Kohli become the fastest player to 14,000 T20 runs and the first Indian with 10 T20 centuries, solidifying his legacy as a supreme chaser.

title: Two more India-bound ships cross Hormuz, taking total to 13
content: Two more LPG carriers, foreign-flagged Symi and NV Sunshine, successfully navigated the Strait of Hormuz, bringing the total India-bound vessels to 13. These shipments, carrying vital cooking gas and crude oil, underscore ongoing efforts to ensure energy security.  The safe passage highlights robust inter-ministerial coordination amidst regional tensions.

title: Anthropic rejects alleged China bid for secret ‘mythos’; triggers US worry
content: A Chinese think tank quietly asked Anthropic for access to Mythos, its powerful new AI model that can find software vulnerabilities on its own. Anthropic refused, and the White House National Security Council reacted with alarm. With Mythos rattling banks, hospitals and the Pentagon, the request has landed days before Trump meets Xi in Beijing, where AI safety is now on the agenda.

title: Cap, blue bag, knife: How elderly man posing as mechanic terrorised Delhi neighbourhoods
content: A 55-year-old man, Ajay Bagga, has been apprehended by Delhi Police for a series of robberies in Keshav Puram and Pitampura. Posing as a water purifier mechanic, Bagga used a knife and axe-like weapon in two violent incidents, causing panic. He was arrested after a manhunt involving CCTV analysis and technical surveillance.

title: After hantavirus, now norovirus? How a gut bug forced a France cruise ship into lockdown
content: French authorities have eased restrictions on the cruise ship Ambition after confirming a viral gastrointestinal outbreak, not hantavirus, sickened dozens. Around 80 passengers and crew experienced symptoms, with no severe cases reported. A passenger's death was attributed to a heart attack, unrelated to the illness. The ship, carrying over 1,700 people, was temporarily restricted as a precaution.

title: Rupee inches towards 96: Currency touches record low at 95.85 against US dollar
content: The Indian Rupee hit a fresh all-time low against the US dollar, slumping to 95.74 amid soaring crude oil prices and Middle East tensions.  While RBI interventions and higher gold import duties offered some support, the currency remains vulnerable.  Foreign investors offloaded equities, yet domestic stock markets opened higher.

title: America's biggest Fintech startup files for bankruptcy; CEO shares 6 'mistakes' to not repeat
content: Fintech startup Parker, known for its corporate credit cards and banking services for e-commerce businesses, has filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy. Despite raising over $200 million, the company reportedly shut down abruptly after acquisition talks failed. This leaves its small business customers in a difficult situation regarding access to funds.

title: Karnataka revokes 2022 order, allows hijab in schools, colleges
content: Karnataka has withdrawn its 2022 dress code order, now permitting students to wear religious symbols like hijabs, turbans, and sacred threads alongside uniforms. This new policy, effective from the next academic year for classes 1-12, aims to balance institutional discipline with individual freedoms. The BJP criticized the move as "vote-bank appeasement."

title: Manish Pandey pulls off a wordie; Virat Kohli's reaction goes viral - Watch
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru moved top of the IPL 2026 table after beating Kolkata Knight Riders by six wickets in Raipur. Manish Pandey stole attention with a stunning one-handed catch to dismiss Tim David, leaving players shocked. However, Virat Kohli dominated the match with an unbeaten 105, becoming the fastest batter to reach 14,000 T20 runs and guiding RCB home comfortably.

title: LinkedIn to lay off hundreds of employees 'as part of regular business planning'
content: Microsoft-owned LinkedIn is reportedly laying off approximately 875 employees, or 5% of its global workforce, across its Global Business Organization, marketing, engineering, and product teams. The company is also reducing spending on marketing, vendor costs, and office space as part of its strategic realignment for future growth and profitability.

title: 'US, China should be partners not rivals': Key takeaways from Trump and Xi's much-watched summit
content: US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping met at the Great Hall of the People, striking a warm and optimistic tone. Both leaders emphasized cooperation over confrontation, despite existing tensions. Trump praised the reception and Xi's leadership, while Xi framed the meeting as a defining moment for global stability and called for partnership.

title: IPL Playoff Scenarios: KKR crash to 2.6% - odds for each team explained
content: With 13 league-stage games remaining, GT and RCB are almost assured of playoff spots, while SRH and PBKS remain well placed. CSK and RR have slightly better-than-even chances of making the top four, but KKR and DC are nearly out. Among 8,192 possible result combinations, GT and RCB also hold the strongest chances of finishing in the top two.

title: Scrap NEET, allow states to fill medical seats based on Class 12 marks, CM Vijay writes to Centre
content: Following the NEET-UG 2026 cancellation due to paper leak allegations, Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay urged the Union government to scrap NEET. He advocated for state-level admissions based on Class 12 marks for MBBS, BDS, and AYUSH seats, citing the exam's flaws and its disadvantage to rural and government school students.

title: Heavy rain and dust storm leave 33 dead across Uttar Pradesh
content: A devastating storm swept through five districts of Uttar Pradesh, claiming at least 33 lives and causing widespread destruction. Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath has ordered immediate relief and compensation for affected families, with district authorities tasked to expedite assistance. Surveys are underway to assess damage to crops, livestock, and homes, as rescue efforts faced disruptions due to the severe weather.

title: Bandi Sanjay’s son skips Pocso summons; ‘missing’ posters surface
content: Union minister's son, Sai Bageerath, failed to appear before Cyberabad police for questioning in a Pocso case. Investigators have deployed teams to locate him, indicating an imminent arrest. A purported email seeking a delay was dismissed by police, who are also examining discrepancies in the survivor's birth records.

title: 3 killed, including child, in Delhi’s Alipur hit-and-run; dumper driver held
content: A tragic hit-and-run in outer Delhi's Alipur claimed the lives of three individuals, including a six-year-old girl, and left four others seriously injured. A dumper truck allegedly rammed into a shared autorickshaw, with the driver later apprehended based on CCTV footage. The victims were returning from Uttar Pradesh when the accident occurred.

title: India bans sugar exports till Sept 30 amid supply concerns
content: India has banned sugar exports until September 30 to control domestic prices amid fears of lower production. This move is expected to impact global supplies and boost international rates. While some shipments already in transit will be allowed, traders face challenges fulfilling existing contracts. The decision follows concerns over weakening cane yields and potential monsoon disruptions.

title: 'Home Alone' at White House: Vance jokes as Trump visits China for key talks
content: Vice President J.D. Vance likened his solitary White House presence to Macaulay Culkin in "Home Alone" due to Secret Service rules preventing him from traveling with President Trump. Vance also cautioned that federal funding for state health insurance programs could be halted if they don't cooperate with the administration's fraud crackdown.

title: Hantavirus outbreak: Deadly Andes strain detected in cruise ship cases; 8 cases confirmed
content: A hantavirus outbreak linked to the cruise ship MV Hondius has seen eight passengers test positive for the Andes virus, a strain known to spread between humans. Three lives have been lost so far. While the risk to the general public is low, passengers and crew are under quarantine for 42 days as investigations continue.

title: Can Vande Bharat sleeper trains be a game-changer for Indian Railways?
content: Over 250 new Vande Bharat sleeper trains are in the pipeline for rollout over the next few years - all with the aim to revamp long-distance rail travel and offer a world-class service experience to passengers.

title: 'I told you so': Kohli proves Krunal Pandya right with century against KKR
content: Virat Kohli made a strong comeback with his ninth IPL century. He guided Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a six-wicket victory over Kolkata Knight Riders. Kohli scored an unbeaten 106 off 60 balls. This win strengthened RCB's position in the IPL 2026 points table. Krunal Pandya had predicted Kohli's return to form.

title: Top stocks to buy: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Top stock market recommendations: Grasim Industries, Hindustan Unilever, and National Aluminium Co have been shortlisted as the top stocks to buy today (May 14, 2026) by Aakash K Hindocha, Deputy Vice President - WM Research, Nuvama Professional Clients Group. The expert also shares his outlook for Nifty, and Bank Nifty.

title: Virat Kohli reveals why the celebration wasn't a 'big one' after scoring 100
content: Virat Kohli smashed an unbeaten 105 to guide Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a six-wicket win over Kolkata Knight Riders in IPL 2026. Chasing 193 in Raipur, RCB won with five balls left as Kohli bounced back from two successive ducks. He called “pressure a privilege” after the match, while captain Rajat Patidar praised the team’s collective effort as RCB moved top of the table.

title: Prateek Yadav dead: 6 ‘unexplained injuries before death' raise questions
content: Prateek Yadav's postmortem revealed a massive lung blood vessel blockage caused his heart and respiratory system to collapse. Doctors preserved his heart, lung clot, and viscera for forensic and chemical analysis to determine the clot's origin and any contributing factors. Six antemortem injuries were also noted, with their nature yet to be conclusively determined.

title: 75 days of Hormuz chaos: Oil prices remain high as Trump-Xi meet
content: Oil prices saw a slight uptick as traders awaited crucial talks between US President Trump and China's Xi Jinping. Lingering concerns over the Strait of Hormuz disruptions and the ongoing Middle East conflict are keeping markets on edge. Despite US pressure, China's continued reliance on Iranian oil adds complexity to potential resolutions.

title: Kohli scripts history with unbeaten ton, becomes fastest ever to...
content: Virat Kohli etched his name in T20 history, becoming the fastest to 14,000 runs in just 409 innings. He celebrated this milestone with a match-winning unbeaten 105, his ninth IPL century, guiding Royal Challengers Bengaluru to a crucial victory. This stellar performance silenced recent critics and reaffirmed his status as a T20 legend.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi earns new fan in Kagiso Rabada: 'There’s not an ounce of fear'
content: South African pacer Kagiso Rabada lauded 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's fearless batting, calling him a rare talent with power and natural flair. Sooryavanshi has impressed by taking on elite bowlers. Rabada, enjoying a strong IPL season himself, emphasized his focus on outsmarting every batter, regardless of their reputation, highlighting Sooryavanshi as an 'X-factor' player.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' box office collection day 56: Ranveer Singh’s film adds Rs 0.37 crore to the tally
content: Ranveer Singh's spy drama 'Dhurandhar 2' has completed 56 days at the box office, amassing over Rs 1,796 crore worldwide. The film added Rs 0.37 crore on Wednesday, with its India net collections reaching Rs 1,144.21 crore. The movie is also slated for an international OTT debut on May 14, 2026.

title: UAE denies reports of secret visit by Netanyahu; Iran says ‘collusion’ is ‘unforgivable’
content: The UAE has officially denied reports of a secret visit by Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu or an Israeli military delegation during the recent Israel-Iran conflict. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs stated that all UAE-Israel ties are transparent under the Abraham Accords, dismissing claims of unannounced arrangements.

title: 'This is the right time': Pakistan consider bringing back Babar Azam as Test captain
content: Masood has led Pakistan in Tests since December 2023. Under him, Pakistan have lost 11 of their 15 matches.

title: Hard copy of leaked NEET-UG paper sourced in Pune, shared on WhatsApp before exam: Probe
content: The CBI arrested five individuals, including three from a Jaipur family, in connection with the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. The arrests followed the CBI taking over the probe from Rajasthan Police, focusing on a family with multiple past NEET qualifiers. Investigators are examining how the alleged leaked material was distributed.

title: Kerala CM announcement LIVE: AICC likely to announce the name today; Congress MLA-elects reach Indira Bhavan for meeting
content: This decision to announce the chief ministerial pick on May 14 came after an almost 40-minute meeting between Rahul Gandhi and Congress chief Mallikarjun Kharge at the latter’s residence

title: ‘Union Government should include enhanced quota under 9th schedule’
content: Karnataka’s Public Works Minister Satish Jarkiholi, who belongs to the Nayaka community, a scheduled tribe, spoke to The Hindu

title: ST quota: Why Congress and BJP are wary of raising the pitch in Karnataka
content: Leadership tussle seems to have held back the Congress while the BJP is grappling with divided opinions and competing interests on the issue of ST quota

title: Kashmir MLA ‘boycotts’ L-G’s drug campaign after demolition drive in his constituency
content: “For decades, people in J&K saw drugs and terrorism as separate threats. But they are not two problems; they are the two heads of the same snake,” said L-G Sinha

title: Supreme Court directs States to install panic buttons, tracking devices in public transport vehicles
content: Fitness certificates or permits should not be issued to public service vehicles found in violation of these safety measures, apex court directs

title: Congress MLA-elects make a beeline for Indira Bhavan as AICC inches closer to announcing its Chief Minister pick for Kerala
content: Congress workers trickled into party offices in the State, as their eagerness to know the party high command’s choice for the top post appeared to reach a feverish pitch

title: 50 years of Annakili: The film that launched Ilaiyaraaja’s career
content:
Fifty years have passed like a fleeting moment, but the songs of Annakili continue to haunt generations of music lovers. With the film’s release began the extraordinary musical journey of Ilaiyaraaja, whose career would later rise to unparalleled heights.

Gangai Amaran, who worked closely with his brother during the composing sessions and served as the rhythm guitarist for the film, recalls those memorable days with B. Kolappan.

“The higher octaves in the humming of the song ‘Annakili Unnai Theduthey’ symbolically foreshadowed the great heights Ilaiyaraaja was destined to achieve,” he said.

title: Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrives in India, to attend BRICS FM meet
content: Araghchi will be coming face to face with UAE Minister of State for Foreign Affairs on Thursday (May 14, 2026) at BRICS Foreign Ministers summit

title: ST quota back at 3% raises concerns over  proportional representation
content: While the 1931 census put ST population at about 0.8%, with more tribes added to the list, it is now estimated to be over 7% of Karnataka’s population

title: Vijay Narayan earns rare distinction of being Advocate General under two different governments
content: The government led by TVK appoints him as the top most law officer of the State; he had held the office under the AIADMK government too between 2017 and 2021

title: Himanta welcomes Bengal cabinet’s decision to hand over land to BSF for border fencing
content: Mr. Sarma said that many organisations in Assam demand that the border must be sealed in the State, but this was meaningless without a similar closure in West Bengal

title: Annakili@50: The extraordinary journey of maestro Ilaiyaraaja
content: If there had been no Panchu Arunachalam, there would have been no Ilaiyaraaja or Gangai Amaran. He was our first god. He lit the light in our lives, says Gangai Amaran, younger brother of Ilaiyaraaja, who was the rhythm guitarist for the film

title: Woman gang-raped in sleeper bus in New Delhi
content: The two accused have been arrested in connection with the incident that took place on May 12, the sources said

title: India bans sugar exports till September 30
content: The notification issued by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) on May 13 said this order does not apply to sugar being exported to the European Union and the U.S. under the tariff rate quota scheme

title: 4 killed, 10 injured as bus catches fire after colliding with tanker in Gujarat's Surendranagar
content: There were around 40 passengers in the bus, and several of them were rescued, Deputy Superintendent of Police said.

title: Karnataka’s Leader of Opposition R Ashok to reduce escort vehicles, travel by Namma Metro
content: Karnataka’s Leader of Opposition R. Ashok announced that he and his family would abide by austerity measures

title: Russia to fulfil all agreements on energy supply to India: FM Lavrov
content: Stating that 'Hindi-Rusi bhai bhai’ (Indians and Russians are brothers), Mr. Lavrov said that's not just a fun slogan to chant, it has become part of our culture

title: Rajasthan CM seeks speedy completion of projects under Yamuna water pact
content: Mr. Sharma met his Haryana counterpart Nayab Singh Saini and Union Jal Shakti Minister C.R. Patil during his visit to New Delhi and deliberated on a timeline for laying the pipelines

title: Iran working on Hormuz ‘protocol’ to cover ‘costs’, says Deputy Foreign Minister Gharibabadi
content: Hinting at UAE, Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi says “a neighbouring country that is also a member of the BRICS” is trying to scuttle a joint statement by seeking condemnation of Iran

title: Activists rue budget cut for promoting millet cultivation in Odisha
content: Right To Food campaign allege the government has reduced funding and withdrawn ground-level support agencies for millet mission

title: Congress high command defers announcement of Kerala CM designate to May 14
content: High command asks Congress MLAs-elect  to report to Indira Bhavan in Thiruvananthapuram, even as Congress workers congregate in front of the houses of Venugopal, Chennithala and Satheesan in anticipation of the momentous announcement from New Delhi

title: West Bengal CM gives sanction to CBI to prosecute officials in several scams
content: Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced that the policy of the previous government relating to a ban on the sale of potatoes to other states stands lifted with immediate effect

title: NSUI holds protest outside Education Minister’s residence over NEET paper leak
content: The protest march highlighted that the paper leak had “shattered the dreams of millions of students”, demanding immediate resignation of Union Minister Dharmendra Pradhan

title: In 2024, NTA and Govt. did not want to cancel NEET, cited student welfare in Supreme Court
content: Two years ago, the government had argued that cancelling the exam would be like treating “unequals equally” and that innocents and wrongdoers would be made to suffer same consequences

title: A history of rebellions within the AIADMK
content: Rebellions are not new to the AIADMK, with the party having seen similar disruptions over the years. Here’s a timeline of events that track dissent within the party since its formation in 1972

title: Nicobarese oppose proposal for three wildlife sanctuaries
content: Tribal Council says Centre did not consult residents of Little Nicobar, Meroe, and Menchal islands, where the sanctuaries are being planned

title: ‘Limited’ religious symbols to be allowed with uniforms in Karnataka schools and colleges
content: The May 13 order revokes the previous BJP government’s February 5, 2022, directive, which had required students to wear prescribed uniforms and had become the basis for restrictions that later triggered the hijab row

title: U.P. Assembly suspends parliamentary committees’ study tours till further orders
content: The orders issued by the Assembly Secretariat’s Principal Secretary, Pradeep Kumar Dubey, have come into immediate effect, officials said

title: DMK boycotts floor test; urges CM to continue with Dravidian model welfare schemes
content: Opposition Leader Udhayanidhi Stalin asks the new government to continue the DMK government’s schemes

title: Assam feels safer with BJP in power in West Bengal: Himanta Biswa Sarma
content: A day after taking oath as the Chief Minister for the second straight term, he says the political alignment between the two neighbouring States would improve coordination to face shared challenges

title: Assam ships first legal agarwood chips to West Asia
content: Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates receive the consignment, unlocking an industrial potential of ₹50,000 crore

title: How the anti-defection law is going to operate in the AIADMK case?
content: There are two grounds for disqualification in the event of defection — when a member voluntarily gives up membership of his or her political party and when a member votes or abstains from voting contrary to any direction issued by his or her political party

title: Three held; security beefed up in Bhopal as Muslim community protests assault on man over ‘love jihad’
content: A large number of Muslim community members, led by city Qazi Sayed Mushtaq Ali Nadwi, gathered near Hamidia hospital on May 12 and later marched to the Police Commissioner’s office to demonstrate

title: Odisha CM reduces number of vehicles in his convoy by half
content: Responding PM Narendra Modi’s call for controlled use of oil in wake of West Asia crisis, CM Mohan Charan Majhi advises people to use public transport

title: BJP, Congress attack SP over party spokesperson’s remark on Brahmins; FIR registered
content: Bhati offers unconditional apology, claims certain parts of his speech were selectively edited to spread ‘malicious propaganda’

title: Two locals, including teacher, held for ‘providing logistic support’ to Saifullah terror group in Kishtwar
content: The police said these locals “extended support to the Saifullah group”, comprising around six non-local terrorists who were neutralised in multiple operations in the Kishtwar district in the past one year

title: ATS questions 57 in Maharashtra over alleged gangster network links
content: Intelligence inputs received over a period of time indicated that gangsters operating from across the border were using social media to recruit youths from Maharashtra, say officials

title: The ‘cinematisation’ of politics
content: The youth don’t necessarily crave for a change — what they crave for is a narrative; some purpose that empowers them with direction

title: GCDA contests government order allotting land adjoining Kaloor stadium to college
content: Agency says Revenue dept. lacked authority to allot its land to college without consulting it; college dismisses GCDA’s contention as baseless, citing a govt. order establishing its ownership of the land

title: 24x7 control rooms for disaster management to be set up in Ernakulam
content: The centres will be set up as part of the district’s monsoon preparedness measures to handle rain-related incidents. The monsoon is expected to reach the Kerala coast early this year, by May 24

title: Congress unveils ‘Bhu Guarantee’
content: The announcements include a reduction in the guidance value cost that needs to be paid for conversion from B-Khata to A-Khata, and regularisation of buildings in BDA areas constructed before 2008

title: Ilaiyaraaja’s trendsetter movie Annakili turns 50
content: A black-and-white film running successfully for 200 days was remarkable, and much of that achievement was due to the songs composed by Ilaiyaraaja, says actor Sivakumar

title: Bengaluru: Nearly 80% land acquisition done for PRR Package 1, work order to be issued in June
content: This will be the first on-ground work to begin for PRR, which has been delayed for over two decades.

title: Iran war shows military force alone cannot be solution to conflicts: T.S. Tirumurti
content: The former Ambassador and Permanent Representative of India to the United Nations was speaking at the inauguration of Diplomacy and Sustainability Dialogues 2026

title: India should have stepped into vacuum left by U.S. as guarantor of security in West Asia: Talmiz Ahmad
content: Speaking on the topic ‘India’s Diplomatic Balancing in West Asia: Strategic Autonomy, Energy Security and Global Responsibility’, the former Ambassador remarked that “India is not a serious role-player as far as West Asia and the crisis over the last two and a half years is concerned.”

title: Bengal CM Adhikari retains Bhabanipur seat, says wont let people of Nandigram feel his absence
content: CM Suvendu Adhikari joined an “Abhinandan Yatra” in Bhabanipur with other BJP leaders, stating it was a procession to thank people of the constituency, as directed by State BJP president Samik Bhattacharya

title: Tamil Nadu jewellers to stop sale of gold coins
content: Association says the measure aims to help reduce gold imports, asks government to immediately ban ETFs, digital gold sales and restrict gold importers, channelling agents and bullion dealers

title: Learn from Sri Lanka’s experience on impact of fertilizer supply chains: experts
content: The experts participating in a panel discussion on the topic ‘Impact of West Asia Crisis on Economic Disruptions and Sustainability: Implications for India’ say India should continue to follow the current policies while focussing on a transition to sustainable practices in the long term

title: CBSE Class 12 results: Tamil Nadu registers 95.25% pass; girls outperform boys
content: As many as 46,901 boys and 41,245 girls from Tamil Nadu wrote the CBSE Class 12 exams, wherein girls outperformed boys (94%), with a pass rate of 96.68%

title: TDB signs pact with KITSFRA for complete digitisation of temples, offices
content: Temple administration, file movement in offices, financial transactions in temples and offices, services for devotees and various online facilities connected to the Sabarimala temple will be brought under a digital platform

title: Telangana govt. advises people to take adequate precautions in view of scorching heat
content: Revenue Minister asks district collectors to take necessary measures to avoid loss of life or property

title: Trump China Visit LIVE Updates: Trump Offers Praise As Xi Warns Of Confrontation Over Taiwan At Summit
content: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

title: US Jury Finds 'Chinese Agent' Guilty Of Running 'Secret Police Station'
content: Federal prosecutors in Brooklyn said Lu Jianwang, 64, should have alerted the US Attorney General that he was a Chinese agent.

title: Woman Gang-Raped Inside Bus In Delhi, Driver And Conductor Arrested
content: According to the complaint, the woman alleged that two men raped her inside the bus. The bus driver and conductor, have been arrested

title: Cuba Runs Out Of Fuel Amid Iran Crisis, 20-Hour Power Cuts Follow
content: Cuba has installed 1,300 megawatts of solar power over the past two years, but much of that capacity is lost to grid instability amid fuel shortages.

title: Russia Attacks Ukraine's Kyiv With Barrage Of Drones, At Least 4 Injured
content: Damage was recorded across six districts of Kyiv, according to the head of Kyiv's Military Administration Tymur Tkachenko.

title: 6 Months Jail Or Rs 1,000 Fine: New Animal Slaughter Rules In Bengal
content: As per the notification, no person shall slaughter any animal (bulls, bullocks, cows, calves, male and female buffalos, buffalo calves and castrated buffalos), unless a certificate declares it fit for slaughter.

title: Kerala CM Announcement LIVE Updates: Kerala To Get New Chief Minister Today After Days Of Congress-Led UDF Deadlock
content: Kerala Chief Minister Live Updates: A formal announcement is expected today, once Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge returns to Delhi and holds further talks with senior figures, including Sonia Gandhi.

title: Iran War, Oil To Dominate BRICS Summit As Foreign Ministers Reach Delhi
content: Iranian Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi arrived in New Delhi late Wednesday, Iran's embassy in India said.

title: Delhi Cuts Official Vehicles, Foreign Trips, Pushes EVs After PM's Appeal
content: Under the new policy, one day every week will be observed as "No Car Day", with officers and staff encouraged to use public transport or shared transport systems for commuting.

title: Prateek Yadav Had 6 Injuries Before Death, Clot In Lung Killed Him: Autopsy
content: Prateek Yadav's autopsy report mentioned injuries to his chest, under his right arm, right forearm, right elbow joint, over the elbow and left wrist.

title: US Senate Confirms Trump Nominee Kevin Warsh As Federal Reserve Chair
content: The Senate voted 54 to 45 to confirm Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair, who like Trump has advocated for lower interest rates.

title: PhD Student Of BITS Pilani Goa Dies By Suicide, 7th Case In 18 Months
content: Arjun Sabu, a second-year PhD student at the institute, was found hanging late Tuesday night, police said.

title: Is The IPO Euphoria Over? Why 66% Of New Listings Slip Below Issue Price
content: Share Market News: FY26 saw a record Rs 1.79 lakh crore raised via 108+ IPOs. Yet, nearly 66 per cent of these stocks now trade below issue price.

title: Rubio Calls China 'Most Important' US Partner As Trump-Xi Hold Summit
content: Rubio also said he was sure that Trump and Xi would discuss Taiwan, the self-governing democratic island claimed by Beijing.

title: HC’s vote curb on TVK MLA ‘atrocious to say the least’: SC
content: a bench of justices stayed the order of the madras high court against the govt . HC says remedy is election petition and still entertains the writ petition . the supreme court accepted the plea of senior advocate Abhishek Manu Singhvi .

title: Won’t revisit ‘Hinduism a way of life’ ruling, SC says in Sabarimala case
content: a nine-judge bench said it would not revisit SC’s three-decade-old ruling of “Hinduism is a way of life” . the bench said the court should not be the forum to determine what is an ideal religious practice .

title: Manipur on boil again as 3 clerics killed and 4 wounded in ambush
content: three church leaders killed and four others injured in ambush by unidentified armed men . the attack occurred around 10:25am on the stretch locally known as Tiger Road, between Kotzim and Kotlen villages . all three died on the spot; four others sustained grievous injuries .

title: Venugopal, Satheesan or Chennithala? Suspense on Kerala CM to end today
content: all three aspirants strongly pressed their claims before the Congress leadership of Mallikarjun Kharge and Rahul Gandhi . he is widely viewed as the man who spearheaded the party and UDF charge during the five years of the left front govt, which won a repeat term in 2021 .

title: Delhi Police arrest influencer over social media post on businessman Sunjay Kapur’s death
content:
NEW DELHI: Delhi Police on Wednesday arrested a social media influencer, following a court order. The suspect, who went by the handle @skindoctor13, was accused in a case registered against him around two months ago for a post on X about the death of businessman Sunjay Kapur. He was called in for questioning and subsequently placed under arrest.

End of Article

title: Why MP’s rising tiger toll is more than a ‘core’ issue
content: the state has lost 32 tigers in the first five months of 2026 . a canine distemper virus killed a tigress and 4 cubs in the state . the real story behind the rising big cat toll may lie outside their protected boundaries .
